subject: [SO] SOL003 adapter,bpmn-infra, nfvo HTTPs to HTTP
commit_message:
[SO] SOL003 adapter,bpmn-infra, nfvo HTTPs to HTTP

Removing https communication as SO SOL003 Adapter has used its own keystore for communications with bpmn-infra and etsi-nfvo
with hardcoded password.Common way of certicates will break existing communication so going for HTTP path due to time constraint .
Will revisit security in Honululu as part of https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-3350 epic.

Change-Id: I521d9be9007f229919fb5d88f476a692faae10e1
Issue-ID: SO-3349
Signed-off-by: Mukesh <mukeshsharma@est.tech>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

@Sylvain,as Mukesh explained in his email to you...


This is in reference to your review https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/114367/4/kubernetes/so/components/so-vnfm-adapter/resources/config/overrides/override.yaml
 
The Ericsson team has discussed internally about the above hardcoded password. The reason that we put keystore and truststore passwords in the SOL003 Adapter is that the SOL003 Adapter has used its own keystore for communications with bpmn-infra and etsi-nfvo.
 
If we are using the common way of certificates as you are changing, it will break existing communications between bpmn-infra/etsi-nfvo and SOL003 Adapter.
 
As we reported it to you, due to above changes in OOM, we are not able to successfully test the communications around the SOL003 Adapter.
 
Since we don’t have much time to complete our tests for ETSI-Alignment (it used to work but after OOM changes, no), at this time, out team would like to go with the HTTP path instead of HTTPS, as part of this review for Guilin. Another rationale for the HTTP path would be the SO NFVO, SOL003 Adapter and BPMN-Infra are part of SO. In Guilin ,we can treat their communications as SO internal, where HTTPS support is not mandatory. For Guilin, this HTTP path would simplify current issues around the SOL003 Adapter.
 
Security is important. In Honolulu, we will revisit these security work items, as part of https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-3350 epic. Thanks.

Could you please let us know your view and advise. 

BR,
Byung

Patch Set 2:

Hello, as said by email, I believe the patch I'm providing, when working, should solve the hardcoded certificate.
As far as I understand the way it's done, communication should continue to work after the patch.
so this patch would be not necessary (I hope ^_^)

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

@Sylvain, first of all, thank you!! If you are sure your patch resolves the communication issues we are experiencing, our team will bring up new deployment with the latest OOM code next Monday and retest ETSI-Alignment test cases. 

Then, we will let you know the test outcome. If it does not work, you can tweak your patch to make SOL003 Adapter communications work, or going for the HTTP path. We will test it first. 

Let's discuss next Monday. Thanks!
